TextView.android:text: Plugin Manager

Install .ptp or .zip plugin packages into /sdcard/dev.perms.test/plugins. PermsTest supports declarative JSON UI plugins, controlled shell/script plugins, trusted native handlers, and a trusted-Dex type that is limited to explicit user-tapped dispatch after runtime-gate, exact-payload trust, SHA-256, capability, and class/method checks. Declarative UI shell buttons share the same shell/script policy gates and are blocked in Preview UI. Use a plugin card menu to review or save runtime policy details, including trusted-Dex payload hashes and loader-readiness status when declared, before running actions. Refresh List rereads staged plugins. Restore Selected installs checked bundled plugins; bundled choices start unchecked so you only restore what you select. Select Plugin only fills the path field; Import Plugin picks and installs in one step.
Plugin Runtime Policy

Controlled script actions are user-tapped and routed through the existing shell capture backend. Optional per-plugin script approval can require Review Runtime Policy approval before script actions run, and optional per-run confirmation can show the exact script review before every tap. Trusted-Dex loading is explicit-tap only and requires the runtime gate, exact-payload trust, declared SHA-256 match, capabilities, and class/method metadata checks; optional per-run trusted-code confirmation can show the exact trusted-code review before dispatch. Plugin capabilities and action requires metadata are parsed, checked when declared, and reviewable/savable from each plugin card menu. Reviews include script file summaries for script-capable plugins, trusted-Dex payload path/hash/class metadata, exact-payload trust status, and loader-readiness checks for trusted code candidates.

com.google.android.material.checkbox.Mat.android:text: Require Review Runtime Policy approval for script plugins
Confirm each shell/script action before running
Enable trusted-Dex plugin loading (explicit trusted taps only)
Confirm each trusted-code action before running

TextView.android:text: Runtime policy not loaded yet.
Bundled Plugins

Choose which built-in plugins to restore from /assets/plugins. Items start unchecked; checked items are installed or refreshed when you tap Restore Selected. Use Select All/None or Select Demos to quickly choose only the bundled demo/test plugins.
External Plugins

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.android:hint: Selected plugin package, path, folder, or content URI

TextView.android:text: Plugins load after the main app is ready. Disabled plugins stay staged but do not run actions. Declarative plugins use plugin.json plus ui.json. Plugin packages define windowStyle/windowFit defaults; the menu can apply a user large-window override. Long-press a plugin card or use ☰ > Edit Plugin Config to load it into Plugin Editor. Use ☰ > Check Package Readiness before export/import round-trip testing, and use ☰ > Export Plugin Package to repackage any valid staged plugin under plugin_exports. Clean Invalid removes staged folders that fail plugin.json validation.
Installed Plugins
Plugin Editor
Create or edit self-contained PermsTest plugins on-device. Long-press a staged plugin, use the plugin menu Edit Plugin Config, or open a .ptp/.zip package directly into the editor.

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.android:hint: Icon path inside plugin, for example icon.png

TextView.android:text: Plugin Actions

Select an action to edit it. Add, update, remove, or reorder actions here, then tap Build JSON to write the structured list into plugin.json. Load JSON Actions imports the current advanced JSON action array without changing other fields.
No action selected.

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.app:helperText: Optional comma-separated capabilities, such as shell_command or host_api.
Optional full payload hash for trusted_dex review/trust records. Hash Trusted Target can fill this from a staged or queued payload.

TextView.android:text: Action type controls dispatch. Choose Script File is available for shell actions. trusted_dex actions require the runtime gate, exact-payload trust, SHA-256 match, capability checks, class/method metadata, and explicit user tap before in-process dispatch; use the trusted-code fields when editing review metadata. Action requires writes optional capability requirements. Presentation, syntax, and window fields are optional action-level overrides; Default or Inherit removes the override.
Managed Plugin Assets

Add general files under assets/ so they are staged and packaged with the plugin. Choose a file, review its plugin-relative path, then tap Add / Replace. Remove marks an existing staged asset for deletion when the plugin is saved. Icon, script, and declarative UI files keep their dedicated controls.
No managed asset selected.

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.android:hint: Asset path inside plugin, for example assets/help.txt

TextView.android:text: Declarative UI Controls

For declarative plugins, select a top-level ui.json control to edit it. Add, update, remove, or reorder controls here, then use Selected Container Contents to edit group/section children or button-row items. Tap Build UI to apply the structured lists. Raw ui.json remains available for advanced fields.
No UI control selected.

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.android:hint: Default text / output text / dropdown values separated by |

TextView.android:text: Use Fill API Mapping after choosing a supported API name to populate common input/output mappings. Action options JSON still supports sequence steps, nested then actions, and advanced mappings; the selected Control action and simple data/output fields override matching keys.
Selected Container Contents

Select a top-level group, section, or buttons control above. Group and section contents use normal declarative controls. A buttons control edits its button-row items. Nested changes update the selected container immediately, then Build UI applies them to raw ui.json.
Select a group, section, or buttons control to edit its contents.

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.android:hint: Nested default / text / dropdown values separated by |
Nested action target / output control ID
Nested action options JSON / advanced mapping

TextView.android:text: Button-row items always use button type and text/action fields. Group and section children support the same control types and action mappings as top-level controls.

Plugin Actions supports mixed declarative, shell, native, and review-only trusted-Dex action definitions, including optional per-action requires capability metadata. Build JSON refreshes capability metadata from the structured action list. Managed Plugin Assets stages optional files under assets/. Declarative UI Controls and Selected Container Contents can be collapsed while keeping raw syntax-highlighted JSON editors and the safe Preview UI test path available.
